species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Weed in open waste places; probably introduced in the Philippines (Merrill 1923). 1-2300m.

species.citation: (Regel) G.Nicholson , Ill. Dict. Gard. 1 (1884) 59, as 'bettzchiana' ; Veldk., Blumea 19 (1971) 169; ≡ Alternanthera ficoidea var. bettzickiana (Regel) Backer , Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 4 (1949) 93, as 'ficoides'; ≡ Telanthera bettzickiana Regel , Index Seminum (St.Petersburg) 1862 (1862) 28. = Alternanthera ficoidea var. versicolor (Lem.) L.B.Sm. & Downs , Fl. Fanerog. Ilha Cardoso 1972 (1972) 63; ≡ Alternanthera ficoidea var. versicolor (Lem.) Backer , Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 4 (1949) 93, as 'ficoides', nom. inval.; ≡ Alternanthera versicolor (Lem.) Hort. 12 (1865) t.440. ≠ Alternanthera ficoidea auct. non R.Br. ex Griseb. ; Moody, Weeds reported in rice in South and Southeast Asia (1989) 273.
species.distribution: S America.

species.citation: (L.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 2 (1891) 537 ; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 94; ≡ Gomphrena brasiliana L. , Amoen. Acad. 4 (1759) 10. = Alternanthera dentata (Moench) Scheygr. , Fl. Suriname 1 (1932) 39; ≡ Gomphrena dentata Moench , Suppl. Meth. (Moench) (1802) 273.

HUNDRED ISLS (photos), LUZON: Abra (photos), Benguet, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao del Norte, Maguindanao del Sur, MINDORO (photos), PANAY.

species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (15-Jan-21): Reported from Java and Singapore, and listed for the Philippines in L.L. Co's original documents that formed the foundation for this checklist, but I have not seen any specimens.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 990 ; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 80.

Montpellier (1912) 205. = Amaranthus hybridus ssp. incurvatus (Timeroy ex Gren. & Godr.) Brenan , Watsonia 4 (1961) 268; ≡ Amaranthus incurvatus Trimen ex Gren. & Godr. , Fl. France Prosp. (1846) 8. = Amaranthus paniculatus L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1763) 1406 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 128; ≡ Amaranthus hybridus ssp. cruentus var. paniculatus (L.) Thell. , Syn. Mitteleur. Fl. 5 (1914) 247 ; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 79. ≠ Amaranthus caudatus auct. non L. ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 193.
species.distribution: America. LUZON, MINDANAO.
species.notes: Luzon to Mindanao. Naturalized in open waste places.

species.citation: Mart. ex Thell. , Fl. Advent. Montpellier (1912) 203.
species.distribution: America, Caribbean.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 991 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 192; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 128; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 78. = Amaranthus spinosus var. circumscissus Thell. , Syn. Mitteleur. Fl. 5 (1914) 269.
species.distribution: America. PALAWAN.
species.notes: Weed in open waste lands, roadsides, fields, gardens, etc., gregarious and abundant along sand bars and margins of streams, 0-1400m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 989 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 128; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 77.

species.notes: Luzon to Mindanao; naturalized in waste places.

(1804) 43 ; Backer, Fl.Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 76.
species.distribution: C & S America. BALABAC, LUZON: Bataan (photos), MINDANAO: Davao del Norte, SIARGAO.
species.notes: Waste places and cultivated grounds (Merrill 1912). Sea level.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 205 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 190; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 127; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 73. = Celosia cristata L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 205 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 127; ≡ Celosia argentea var. cristata (L.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 2 (1891) 541.
species.distribution: Africa. CULION, MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO (photos), PALAWAN.
species.notes: Pantropic ubiquitous weed, wild but non-native, possibly introduced from tropical Africa. Throughout the Philippines at low and medium elevation in the settled areas. Very common along sandy-gravelly river banks, open dry roadsides, fields, gardens, etc.

genera.last_edited: Edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 26 June 2012

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 219 ; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 103.
species.distribution: Africa, America, Australia, Continental Asia, Europe. LUZON: Benguet.
species.notes: Introduced in Java and naturalized in the E part of the island, N Philippines. A casual weed in temperate vegetable gardens, c. 1300-1400m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 3 January 2026

species.citation: (L.) Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 549 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 194; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 129; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 82; ≡ Achyranthes prostrata L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1762) 296. var. prostrata. Distribution: Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, C & S America, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. Notes: Throughout the Philippines. In the settled areas, common in shaded localities, along roadsides, waste places, thickets, plantation forests, forest-edges, secondary forests, often gregarious. 1-1650m. Native. var. lancifolia (Merr.) Backer, Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 4 (1949) 83; ≡ Cyathula lancifolia Merr., PJS 11c (1916) 179; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 129. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BOHOL, LUZON: Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao Oriental, Zamboanga del Sur, SAMAR. Native.
species.distribution: Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, C & S America, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BASILAN, BOHOL, BONGAO, CAMIGUIN, CEBU (photos), CULION, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Albay, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Bulacan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga (photos), Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao de Oro, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR.

species.citation: (Roxb.) Moq. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 13(2) (1849) 236 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 126; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 72; ≡ Celosia polysperma Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 511. = Deeringia polysperma var. pubescens (Schinz) Merr. , EPFP 2 (1923) 126; ≡ Deeringia indica var. pubescens Schinz , Bull. Herb. Boissier, 2nd Sér. 3 (1903) 3; Type: Warburg 11703, Nortern Luzon.
species.distribution: Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. BASILAN, BATANES, CAMIGUIN (photos), CEBU, CORON, GUIMARAS, LUZON: Abra (photos), Albay, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Cavite (photos), Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Norte, NARANJO ISLS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIASI.
species.notes: Much rarer than Deeringia amaranthoides. Thickets, light forests, forest edges. 5-1800m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 6 July 2017

species.citation: (L.) Mosyakin & Clemants , Ukrayins'k. Bot. Zhurn. 59 (2002) 382 ; Fuentes-Bazan et al. 2012; ≡ Chenopodium ambrosioides L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 219 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 189; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 125; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 101.
species.distribution: Neotropics. LUZON: Benguet.
species.notes: Native of the neotropics, introduced in many other regions, as well as ‘throughout’ the Philippines (although now seem to be common only in Benguet at c. 1400m). Invasive species (CABI 2017).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 14 May 2026

species.citation: Mart. , Beitr. Amarantac. (1825) 93.
species.distribution: Neotropics. ROMBLON (photos).
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Now widespread in Malesia. Gregarious mat-forming weed along roadsides, abandoned fields, wastelands, and other similar hot, dry and dusty sites at low elevation.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 224 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 191; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 132; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 95.
species.distribution: Neotropics. PALAWAN.
species.notes: Introduced long ago in Malesia, either cultivated or semi-naturalized. Throughout the settled areas of the Philippines in cultivation, often occurring as an escape. Lowlands to c.1300m.

species.distribution: America. MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental (photos).

Chron., 1st Ser. 1864 (1864) 654; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 132; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1949) 97. Distribution: S America. Notes: Only known from cultivated plants (Pedersen 2000). Cultivated, not naturalized.
species.distribution: America, Caribbean.
